title: Create agent worktree topology and branching strategy
description: Set up git worktrees under ~/.herdr/worktrees/skill-inspector/ for each agent team: agent-security, agent-cli-tui, agent-ci-devops, agent-docs, agent-bugfix, agent-review, agent-roadmap. Each on branch agents/<name>. Document the merge strategy: agent branches → review approval → squash merge to main.
close_reason: Completed: 7 git worktrees + 7 herdr workspaces with work/review tabs established

title: Establish Review Agent workflow with liza-code-review
description: Design and document the review pipeline: (1) Agent commits to feature branch in worktree, (2) Review Agent checks out branch, runs automated gates + liza-code-review protocol, (3) Results posted to agent's review tab, (4) Approved → merge to main. Create a review-agent.sh script that automates: checkout branch, run go vet/test/fmt, produce diff, apply liza-code-review, report verdict.
close_reason: Completed: review pipeline script + herdr review tabs per agent + liza-code-review protocol integration

title: Set up automated QA gates (go vet, go test, gofmt)
description: Establish automated quality gates that run on every commit: go vet ./... (static analysis), go test ./... (unit tests), gofmt -d . (format check). Wire into CI workflows (ci.yml, release.yml) and as pre-commit hooks. This is infrastructure needed for the multi-agent orchestration review pipeline.
close_reason: Completed: .githooks/pre-commit runs go vet/gofmt/go test on every commit; scripts/review.sh provides automated review pipeline

title: B5: Align Go version in CI with go.mod
description: go.mod declares 'go 1.25.1' but both ci.yml and release.yml use 'go-version: 1.21'. Go 1.21 would refuse to build a module declaring 1.25.1. CI is effectively broken. Fix: update CI workflows to use go-version: '1.25' or 'stable'.
close_reason: Closed

title: B4: Add URL fetch size limit and HTTP timeout
description: loader.go:91 calls io.ReadAll(resp.Body) with no size limit — a malicious server can cause OOM. Also http.DefaultClient has no timeout — hangs forever on unresponsive URLs. Fixes: (1) Wrap resp.Body with io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 10<<20) for 10 MiB cap. (2) Create http.Client{Timeout: 10 * time.Second} or use context.WithTimeout. This was planned as TASK-042 but never implemented.
close_reason: Implemented: B2 enhanced stripANSI for all escape types and applied to render pipeline; B4 added 10MiB LimitReader and 10s HTTP client timeout

title: B3: Add --help and --version flags
description: Running skill-inspector with no args prints a one-line usage message. Add standard --help (prints usage + description + examples) and --version (prints version string) flags. Use os.Args parsing or a simple flag check before the input path logic in main.go.
close_reason: Completed: --help, -h, --version, -v flags implemented with build-time version injection via ldflags

title: B2: Sanitize terminal escape sequences in rendered content
description: CRITICAL: Content from inspected skill files is written to the terminal with zero sanitization. Malicious skills can embed ANSI/OSC/DCS escape sequences that hide content, hijack the terminal, or break the TUI. The stripANSI function already exists in tui.go — apply it to all rendered content lines from buildSourceLines before writing to terminal. Also strip OSC sequences (\x1b]), DCS sequences (\x1bP), and other non-SGR escapes.
close_reason: Implemented: B2 enhanced stripANSI for all escape types and applied to render pipeline; B4 added 10MiB LimitReader and 10s HTTP client timeout

title: B1: Create MIT LICENSE file
description: README says MIT but no LICENSE file exists. Repo is legally unlicensed without it. Create a standard MIT LICENSE file at repo root with copyright holder 'parksjr'.
close_reason: Created LICENSE

title: Launch Blockers (P0)
description: 5 critical items gating the open-source launch: LICENSE file, ANSI escape sanitization, --help/--version flags, URL fetch memory bomb fix, and Go version alignment in CI. See LAUNCH-READINESS.md for details.
close_reason: All 5 P0 blockers resolved: B1 LICENSE, B2 ANSI sanitization, B3 --help/--version, B4 URL fetch limits, B5 Go version in CI

title: H8: Fix README install path mismatch
description: README line 25 says binary installs to /usr/local/bin/ but install.sh uses ~/.local/bin (XDG standard). Fix README to match the actual install.sh behavior. While there, add a note about ensuring ~/.local/bin is in PATH.
close_reason: Fixed README install path to ~/.local/bin + added PATH note

title: H7: Remove committed binary and .DS_Store from repo
description: The 8MB skill-inspector binary and .DS_Store (macOS metadata) are tracked in git. Remove both: git rm skill-inspector, git rm .DS_Store. Update .gitignore to prevent re-commit (add /skill-inspector and .DS_Store entries).
close_reason: Closed

title: H6: Fix truncateLine byte-slicing bug
description: tui.go:589 does line[:maxWidth-1] on the raw string (containing multi-byte UTF-8 and ANSI escape codes), not on visible characters. stripANSI correctly computes visible length but the slice operates on unprocessed bytes — can cut through the middle of a multi-byte rune producing invalid UTF-8. Fix: compute the rune-based truncation point after stripping ANSI, then map back to the original string position.
close_reason: Completed: replaced raw byte slicing with rune-aware walk that counts visible characters, skipping ANSI escapes

title: H5: Add go test to CI workflow
description: Both ci.yml and release.yml run 'go vet ./...' but never 'go test ./...'. The two most security-critical packages (parser, colorizer) have zero test coverage. Add 'go test ./...' to both workflows. Consider also adding 'go test -race ./...' for race detection and a gofmt check.
close_reason: Closed

title: H4: Detect YAML directives, multi-doc separators, CDATA, JS/CSS comments
description: Parser gaps: (1) YAML directives like %YAML 1.2 at file start are undetected — can be used to set dangerous parser modes. (2) Multi-document YAML with ... separator allows benign first document + hidden second document. (3) CDATA sections in HTML are not scanned. (4) JavaScript // line comments and CSS /* block */ comments embedded in HTML are not scanned. Each gap is a potential injection vector. Add detection for all four.
close_reason: P1 complete: H1 added bidi chars+tag+VS to suspiciousRunes; H2 changed isURL to https-only; H4 added YAML/CDATA/JS/CSS comment detection

title: H3: Add SHA256 checksum verification to install.sh
description: install.sh downloads binary from GitHub releases with no integrity check. Generated SHA256 checksums in release CI (release.yml), publish alongside binaries, and verify in install.sh before executing. Also fix: wget fallback doesn't check exit code — add explicit error handling.
close_reason: Closed

title: H2: Reject HTTP (plaintext) URLs — accept only HTTPS
description: isURL in loader.go:36 accepts http:// URLs. A MITM attacker on public WiFi can intercept and inject malicious content that passes inspection. Fix: change isURL to only accept https://. Alternatively, print a prominent warning for http:// but default to secure-only for v1.
close_reason: P1 complete: H1 added bidi chars+tag+VS to suspiciousRunes; H2 changed isURL to https-only; H4 added YAML/CDATA/JS/CSS comment detection

title: H1: Add bidi control characters to suspiciousRunes
description: Missing from suspiciousRunes map in parser.go: U+202A-U+202E (bidi overrides/isolates/pop), U+2066-U+2069 (directional isolates). These enable Trojan Source attacks (CVE-2021-42574) where RTL/LTR overrides reorder text visually to hide malicious instructions. Add ~10 entries to the map. Also consider tag characters (U+E0001-U+E007F) and variation selectors.
close_reason: P1 complete: H1 added bidi chars+tag+VS to suspiciousRunes; H2 changed isURL to https-only; H4 added YAML/CDATA/JS/CSS comment detection

title: High Priority Launch Items (P1)
description: 8 high-priority findings strongly recommended before launch: bidi control chars, HTTP rejection, install.sh checksums, YAML/CDATA/comment detection gaps, CI test runner, truncateLine bug fix, repo hygiene, and README accuracy. See LAUNCH-READINESS.md for details.
close_reason: All 8 P1 high-priority items resolved: H1-H8 completed and merged

title: Add unit tests for colorize package (0% coverage)
description: colorize/colorize.go has zero test coverage despite being pure string→string functions. colorizeLine accepts well-defined inputs (headings, code fences, emphasis markers, frontmatter, HTML comments, plain text) and returns predictable ANSI-wrapped output. Add table-driven tests covering: heading detection (# through ######), code fence opening/closing, frontmatter delimiters, HTML comments, emphasis patterns (*word*, _word_, **bold**, __bold__), plain text passthrough, and NoColor mode. Target: 90%+ coverage. This is the easiest package to achieve high coverage on — pure functions, no I/O.

title: M21: Set GitHub repo metadata (description, topics, website)
description: Repo will default to empty description and no topics. Set: description='CLI/TUI tool for inspecting agent skill files for hidden content before installation', topics=['go','cli','tui','security','agent-skills','prompt-injection','code-review'], website pointing to README.
close_reason: Set GitHub repo description, topics (7), and website via gh CLI

title: M20: Configure git remote and create initial tag v0.1.0
description: No git remote configured, zero tags. Set up origin remote pointing to github.com/parksjr/skill-inspector, then create v0.1.0 tag after blockers are resolved.
close_reason: Added git remote origin, created v0.1.0 tag

title: M19: Create CONTRIBUTING.md
description: Standard OSS file covering: how to set up dev environment, how to run tests, code style (gofmt), PR process, and CLA/DCO if applicable.
close_reason: Created CONTRIBUTING.md with dev setup, build/test instructions, code style, PR process, and DCO

title: M18: Create SECURITY.md
description: Critical for a security tool. Should include: security model, supported versions, reporting process (email/GPG key), disclosure policy, and dependency policy (golang.org/x/term rationale).
close_reason: Created SECURITY.md with security model, supported versions, reporting process, disclosure policy, and dependency rationale

title: M17: Add test and fmt targets to Makefile
description: Makefile missing: test target (go test ./...), fmt target (gofmt -w . or goimports), lint target. Add these for developer convenience. Also add to .PHONY.
close_reason: Closed

title: M16: Optimize binary size with -ldflags '-s -w'
description: The committed binary is 8MB. Rebuilding with -ldflags '-s -w' (strip debug symbols + DWARF) typically reduces Go binary size by ~30%. The Makefile already uses these flags — the committed binary appears to have been built without them.
close_reason: Closed

title: M15: Add help overlay (h or ? key) in TUI
description: No in-app help. Add h key that shows a modal overlay listing all keyboard controls and their actions, similar to the install confirmation modal.
close_reason: Completed: h and ? keys show modal overlay with keyboard controls organized by Navigation, Views, Actions

title: M14: Add NO_COLOR / --no-color support
description: No support for NO_COLOR env var or --no-color flag. Accessibility standard for terminal tools. When NO_COLOR is set or --no-color is passed, skip all ANSI colorization (return plain text from ColorizeLines).
close_reason: Completed: NO_COLOR env var and --no-color flag support, refactored arg parsing into parseFlags()

title: M13: Add g/G navigation keys (top/bottom of document)
description: Missing vim-style g (top) and G (bottom) navigation. Standard expectation for terminal pagers. Add to readKey() and the main loop in tui.go.
close_reason: Completed: added g (scroll top) and G (scroll bottom) key bindings

title: M12: Deduplicate ANSI constants between colorize and tui
description: colorize.go defines Bold, Reset, etc. tui.go defines boldOn, resetAll etc. — same escape sequences, different names. Consolidate into one place (colorize package or a shared constants file) and import throughout.
close_reason: All P2 items resolved: M8 emphasis detection, M9 double-unquoting, M10 error masking, M12 ANSI dedup

title: M11: Add Unicode homoglyph detection
description: PRD line 91 calls for Unicode homoglyph detection (characters visually resembling ASCII). Current suspiciousRunes only has invisible/formatting chars. Add scanning for Latin Extended, Greek, Cyrillic, and other ranges where characters look like ASCII (e.g. Cyrillic 'а' vs Latin 'a'). This is a large feature — scope to most common homoglyph ranges for v1.
close_reason: Implemented: M1 config Lstat check, M2 TOCTOU re-verification, M3 copyDir symlink skip, M11 homoglyph detection (33 entries)

title: M10: Fix loadAgentDirs silently masking permission errors
description: loadAgentDirs returns defaultAgentDirs() on os.IsNotExist, but silently falls back to defaults on ANY os.Open error (e.g. permission denied). An unreadable config is a user error that should be reported, not silently ignored.
close_reason: All P2 items resolved: M8 emphasis detection, M9 double-unquoting, M10 error masking, M12 ANSI dedup

title: M9: Fix FrontmatterValue double-unquoting bug
description: parser.go FrontmatterValue strips double quotes then single quotes sequentially. A YAML value "'hello'" (double-quoted string containing literal single quotes) gets incorrectly reduced from 'hello' to hello. Fix: only unquote once based on the outermost quote character.
close_reason: All P2 items resolved: M8 emphasis detection, M9 double-unquoting, M10 error masking, M12 ANSI dedup

title: M8: Fix colorizeLine false positives on * and _
description: colorize.go:89-92 dims ANY line containing * or _. This catches: bullet lists (* Item), math (x * y), snake_case_variables, wildcards, etc. The PRD says 'Bold/italic markers — subtle highlight' meaning markdown syntax (**bold**, __bold__, *italic*, _italic_), not bare punctuation. Fix: check for markdown pairs (** __ * _) not bare characters.
close_reason: All P2 items resolved: M8 emphasis detection, M9 double-unquoting, M10 error masking, M12 ANSI dedup

title: M7: Eliminate double-parsing of skill content
description: deriveSkillName() in loader.go calls parser.Parse(content) only to extract frontmatter 'name', discarding the full ParseResult. Then main.go calls parser.Parse(sf.Content) again — O(2n) for no reason. Fix: either cache ParseResult on SkillFile or extract name after the single parse in main.go.
close_reason: Completed: all fixes verified with tests, go vet, gofmt, QA gates passed

title: M6: Exclude fenced code blocks from HTML comment detection
description: extractHTMLComments scans entire file for <!-- regardless of markdown context. HTML comments inside fenced code blocks (backtick-backtick-backtick) are false positives that train users to ignore findings. Skip fenced code blocks and inline code when scanning.
close_reason: Completed: all fixes verified with tests, go vet, gofmt, QA gates passed

title: M5: Fix keypress goroutine leak on exit
description: readKey goroutine runs infinite loop on unbuffered channel. When Run() returns, goroutine blocks forever on send. Use a done channel or context.Context to signal the goroutine to exit.
close_reason: Completed: all fixes verified with tests, go vet, gofmt, QA gates passed

title: M4: Fix normalizeGitHubBlobURL encoding edge cases
description: normalizeGitHubBlobURL uses parsed.EscapedPath() which preserves percent-encoding. Doubly-encoded path segments can cause raw URL to resolve to a different file than the blob URL. Use parsed.Path (decoded) with url.PathEscape for segment reconstruction.
close_reason: Completed: all fixes verified with tests, go vet, gofmt, QA gates passed

title: M3: Prevent copyDir from following symlinks
description: copyDir uses os.ReadDir and copyFile, both of which follow symlinks. A source directory containing a symlink to /etc/passwd would copy its contents into the install directory. Check entry.Type() for os.ModeSymlink and skip.
close_reason: Implemented: M1 config Lstat check, M2 TOCTOU re-verification, M3 copyDir symlink skip, M11 homoglyph detection (33 entries)

title: M2: Fix TOCTOU race between PlanInstall and Install
description: PlanInstall generates preview, user confirms, then Install runs. Between steps: agent directories could be deleted or replaced with symlinks. Re-verify agent directory existence and non-symlink status at Install time.
close_reason: Implemented: M1 config Lstat check, M2 TOCTOU re-verification, M3 copyDir symlink skip, M11 homoglyph detection (33 entries)

title: M1: Prevent config file symlink attack
description: loadAgentDirs doesn't verify ~/.config/skill-inspector/config is a regular file — an attacker could symlink it to a controlled file. Verify config is a regular file (not symlink) before opening, or use O_NOFOLLOW.
close_reason: Implemented: M1 config Lstat check, M2 TOCTOU re-verification, M3 copyDir symlink skip, M11 homoglyph detection (33 entries)

title: Medium Priority & Polish (P2)
description: 21 medium-priority items across security hardening, code quality, UX polish, and community documentation. These are post-launch or time-permitting. See LAUNCH-READINESS.md for details.
close_reason: All P2 items resolved: M8 emphasis detection, M9 double-unquoting, M10 error masking, M12 ANSI dedup

title: V8: Uninstall command
description: Remove an installed skill and its symlinks cleanly. Could be a subcommand (skill-inspector uninstall <name>) or integrated into the TUI. Listed as future scope in PRD.

title: V7: Skill diff/update detection
description: Detect when an installed skill's remote source has changed. Could show a diff view or simply flag that an update is available. Natural extension of the URL-fetch + install workflow. Not in PRD scope — future roadmap item.

title: V6: Homebrew formula
description: Create a Homebrew formula (Ruby) for macOS distribution: brew install parksjr/tap/skill-inspector. Formula should download the darwin binary from GitHub releases. Lower friction than curl|sh for macOS developers.

title: V5: --dry-run install flag
description: PlanInstall already computes the install preview internally. Expose it: when user presses 'i', show the preview as today, but add a --dry-run flag (or 'd' key) that prints what would happen without writing anything. Essentially just calling PlanInstall and displaying the result.

title: V4: Search (/) in TUI
description: Add / key for forward search and ? for backward search. Standard for terminal pagers (less, vim). Press / to enter search mode, type query, press Enter to jump to first match. n/N for next/previous match.

title: V3: Homoglyph detection (Latin Extended, Greek, Cyrillic)
description: Scan for characters in Latin Extended, Greek, Cyrillic, and other ranges that visually resemble ASCII characters (e.g., Cyrillic 'а' U+0430 vs Latin 'a' U+0061, Greek 'ο' U+03BF vs Latin 'o' U+006F). These are used for domain spoofing and can similarly hide malicious variable names or instructions in skill files. Large scope — consider incremental rollout by script/language.

title: V2: Risk scoring heuristic
description: Add simple risk scoring: count suspicious chars + presence of HTML comments + frontmatter anomalies → low/medium/high risk level. Display in a badge/banner at the top of the TUI and in the --json output. Helps users triage findings. README says 'intentionally human-led: no scoring' — this would be a configurable opt-in, not a replacement for human review.

title: V1: JSON batch/CI output mode (--json)
description: Add --json flag that dumps parse results as structured JSON to stdout and exits (no TUI). Enables CI pipeline integration: skill-inspector --json SKILL.md | jq .suspiciousChars. Should include all ParseResult fields: frontmatter, htmlComments, suspiciousChars. Non-TTY invocation currently errors — this gives it a purpose.

title: Value-Add Features (Post-Launch Roadmap)
description: 8 features beyond PRD scope for future releases: JSON batch/CI mode, risk scoring, homoglyph detection, TUI search, dry-run install, Homebrew formula, skill diff/update, and uninstall command. See LAUNCH-READINESS.md for details.
